claims.name: Compressed random access
claims.meaning: Jump to an arbitrary token while the sequence remains compressed.

claims.name: Search on compressed VRAM data
claims.meaning: Count and locate patterns without expanding the searchable index.

claims.name: Fused compressed matrix compute
claims.meaning: Consume compressed weights inside the matrix operation without a full decompressed buffer.

claims.name: Compressed KV attention
claims.meaning: Hold substantially more context state in the same VRAM budget.

claims.name: Sparse direct access
claims.meaning: Read the few required positions instead of decompressing millions of unused values.

claims.name: Cross-request prompt sharing
claims.meaning: Share common prompt state across 256 near-identical requests.

boundary: These are repository benchmark claims, not a universal prediction. Production claims require exact-workload PASS evidence and independent reproduction.
